description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with a boxy, pixelated design reminiscent of early personal computers. The front features a large, dark rectangular screen framed by a thick, light-colored bezel, with a few small, blocky details suggesting buttons or vents below the display. The sides and rear are stepped and angular, with the back panel displaying vertical slits that likely represent ventilation grilles. The base is integrated into the main body, forming a solid, tiered pedestal that supports the monitor. The overall color scheme is a uniform light gray with some darker accents, and the surface appears matte and hard. No visible branding or ports are present, and the design is simple and utilitarian.
description: A retro, boxy computer monitor with a thick light gray bezel, dark screen, and stepped pedestal base. The rear features vertical ventilation slits and the surface is matte and hard.
description_view_0: Front view shows the large dark screen, thick bezel, and a few small details below the display.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the stepped, angular profile and the solid, tiered base.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals vertical ventilation slits and blocky structural details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the stepped profile and shows the back curvature.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, blocky, and light gray, with a uniform matte finish and sharp, angular corners. It is visually prominent and frames the display area with no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The stand is an integrated, stepped pedestal base made of the same light gray plastic as the monitor, with no visible adjustability or separate components.
attribute_button_placement: A small, blocky button is visible below the screen on the front lower left, separated from other features.
